Dashboard Archive: 2026-04-28 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum-ranker data shows LBRT (Liberty Energy) commanding the #1 position with a composite score of 95.7, driven by extraordinary long-term returns of 171.57% over one year and exceptional consistency across timeframes, including a 49.01% three-month return and perfect passage of all four SMA gates. STLD (Steel Dynamics) ranks second with a 78.23% one-year return and strong near-term acceleration—its one-month return of 32.75% significantly outpaced its one-week return of 3.07%, signaling recent momentum pickup. A key difference emerges in resilience: LBRT's bad_spy_score of 3.94 indicates stronger downside cushioning during market weakness compared to STLD's 1.96, though both maintain bullish technical positioning. LBRT's momentum signal reads clean with no pullback flags, while STLD shows modest outperformance versus SPY and QQQ in the one-month window (20.51% and 15.87% respectively), suggesting sector-specific strength in industrials and materials rather than broad market participation.
